concepts.title: Properties of conjugate of complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of conjugate 

1. $\left( \overset{{}}{\mathop{\overline{z}}}\, \right)=\text{z}$

2. $z+\overset{{}}{\mathop{\overline{z}}}\,=2\text{Re}\left( \text{z} \right)$

3. $\text{z}-\bar{z}=2\text{Im}\left( \text{z} \right)$

4. $\overline{{{z}_{1}}+{{z}_{2}}}=\overline{{{z}_{1}}}+\overline{{{z}_{2}}}$

5. $\overline{{{z}_{1}}-{{z}_{2}}}=\overline{{{z}_{1}}-{{z}_{2}}}$

6. $\overline{{{\text{z}}_{1}}{{\text{z}}_{2}}}=\overline{{{\text{z}}_{1}}}\overline{{{\text{z}}_{2}}}$

7. $\left( \frac{\overline{{{z}_{1}}}}{\overline{{{z}_{2}}}} \right)=\frac{\overline{{{z}_{1}}}}{\overline{{{z}_{2}}}}$

8. If $\text{z}=\bar{z}\Rightarrow \text{z}$ is purely real

  1. If $\text{z}+\bar{z}=0\Rightarrow \text{z}$ is purely imaginary.
  2. $z\bar{z}={{\{\operatorname{Re}(z)\}}^{2}}+{{\{\operatorname{Im}(z)\}}^{2}}$

concepts.title: Properties of modulus of complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of Modulus 

  • $\left| z ight|\ge 0\Rightarrow \left| z ight|=0$ iff $z=0$ and $\left| z ight|>0$ iff $z e 0$
  • $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }-\left| z ight|\le ext{Re}\left( z ight)\le \left| z ight|$ and $-\left| lm(z) ight|\le \left| z ight|$. 
  • $|z|=| ar{z}|-| ar{z}|=|-z|=|- ar{z}|$ 
  • $z ar{z}=|z{{|}^{2}}$ 

In general, $\left| {{z}_{1}}{{z}_{2}}{{z}_{3}}\ldots \ldots {{z}_{n}} ight|=\left| {{z}_{1}} ight| ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left| {{z}_{2}} ight| ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left| {{z}_{3}} ight|\ldots .\left| {{z}_{n}} ight|$

  • $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left| rac{{{z}_{1}}}{{{z}_{2}}} ight|= rac{\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|}{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|} ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left( {{z}_{2}} e 0 ight)$ 
  • $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left| {{z}_{1}}\pm {{z}_{2}} ight|\le \left| {{z}_{1}} ight|+\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|$ 

In particular, if $\left| {{z}_{1}}+{{z}_{2}} ight|=\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|+\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|$, then origin, ${{z}_{1}}$ and ${{z}_{2}}$ are collinear with origin at one of the ends.

  • $\left| {{z}_{1}}\pm {{z}_{2}} ight|\ge ||{{z}_{1}}\left| - ight|{{z}_{2}}\parallel $ 

In particular, if $\left| {{z}_{1}}-{{z}_{2}} ight|=\left| \left| {{z}_{1}}\left| - ight|{{z}_{2}} ight| ight|$, then origin, ${{z}_{1}}$ and ${{z}_{2}}$ are collinear with origin at one of the ends.

  • $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\left| {{z}^{n}} ight|=|z{{|}^{n}}$ 
  • $\left| \left| {{z}_{1}} ight|-\left| {{z}_{2}} ight| ight|\le \left| \left| {{z}_{1}} ight|+\left| {{z}_{2}} ight| ight|$

Thus $\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|+\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|$ is the greatest possible value of $\left| {{z}_{1}}+{{z}_{2}} ight|$ and ||${{z}_{1}}\left| - ight|{{z}_{2}}||$ is the least possible value of $\left| {{z}_{1}}+{{z}_{2}} ight|$

  • ${{\left| {{z}_{1}}\pm {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}=\left( {{z}_{1}}\pm {{z}_{2}} ight)\left( \overline{{{z}_{1}}}\pm \overline{{{z}_{2}}} ight)={{\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}\pm \left( {{z}_{1}}\overline{{{z}_{2}}}+\overline{{{z}_{1}}}{{z}_{2}} ight)$ or

${{\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}\pm 2 ext{Re}\left( {{z}_{1}}\overline{{{z}_{2}}} ight)$

  • ${{ ext{z}}_{1}}\overline{{{ ext{z}}_{2}}}+\overline{{{ ext{z}}_{1}}}{{ ext{z}}_{2}}=2\left| {{ ext{z}}_{1}} ight|\left| {{ ext{z}}_{2}} ight| ext{cos}\left( {{ heta }_{1}}-{{ heta }_{2}} ight)$ where ${{ heta }_{1}}= ext{arg}\left( {{ ext{z}}_{1}} ight)$ and ${{ heta }_{2}}= ext{arg}\left( {{ ext{z}}_{2}} ight)$.
  • ${{\left| {{ ext{z}}_{1}}+{{ ext{z}}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}={{\left| {{ ext{z}}_{1}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{ ext{z}}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}\Leftrightarrow rac{{{ ext{z}}_{1}}}{{{ ext{z}}_{2}}}$ is purely imaginary.
  • ${{\left| {{z}_{1}}+{{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{z}_{1}}-{{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}=2\left\{ {{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}} ight\}$
  • $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{\left| a{{z}_{1}}-b{{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| b{{z}_{1}}+a{{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}}=\left( {{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}} ight)\left( {{\left| {{z}_{1}} ight|}^{2}}+{{\left| {{z}_{2}} ight|}^{2}} ight)$ where a, b $\in $ R.
  • Unimodular: i.e., unit modulus.

If $ ext{z}$ is unimodular then $\left| ext{z} ight|=1$. A unimodular complex number can always be expressed as $ ext{cos} heta + ext{isin} heta , heta \in ext{R}$.

Note $: rac{ ext{z}}{\left| ext{z} ight|}$ is always a unimodular complex number if $ ext{z} e 0$.

concepts.title: Properties of argument of complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of Arguments 

  • $\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{1}}{{z}_{2}} \right)=\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{1}} \right)+\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{2}} \right)+2k\pi (k=0$ or 1 or -1$)$

         In general, $\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{1}}{{z}_{2}}{{z}_{3}}\ldots ..{{z}_{n}} \right)=\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{1}} \right)+\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{2}} \right)+\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{3}} \right)+\ldots +\text{Arg}\left( {{z}_{n}} \right)+2k\pi $ (where $\text{k}\in \text{I}$ )

  • $\text{Arg}\left( \frac{{{z}_{1}}}{{{z}_{2}}} \right)=\text{Arg}{{z}_{1}}-\text{Arg}{{z}_{2}}+2k\pi$ (k=0 or 1 or -1$)$
  • $\text{ Arg}\left( \frac{\text{z}}{{\bar{z}}} \right)=2\text{Argz}+2\text{k}\pi (\text{k}=0]$ or 1 or -1)
  • $\text{ Arg}\left( {{\text{z}}^{\text{n}}} \right)=\text{nArgz}+2\text{k}\pi$ (k=0 or 1 or -1$)$
  • If $\text{Arg}\left( \frac{{{z}_{2}}}{{{z}_{1}}} \right)=\theta$, then $\text{Arg}\left( \frac{{{z}_{1}}}{{{z}_{2}}} \right)=2k\pi -\theta$ where $k\in I$
  • $\text{ Arg}\bar{z}=-\text{Argz}$
  • If $\text{arg}\left( \text{z} \right)=0\Rightarrow \text{z}$ is real

Note: Proper value of $k$ must be chosen so, that R.H.S. of (i), (ii), (iii), (iv) lies in $\left( -\pi ,\pi \right]$. All the above formulae are written on the basic of principal argument.
